target.name: Parallel Systems
target.founder: Matt Soule, John Howard, Ben Stabler

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $2.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Transportation. Range: $270M - $1.8B.

data.narrative:
Parallel Systems is a Series B transportation company founded by Matt Soule, John Howard, and Ben Stabler that has raised $100M to date. The valuation assessment relies on a private-company comp universe of 8 peers, including adjacent-stage transportation companies such as Einride (Series C, $1.8B), Harbinger (Series C, $500M), and REGENT Craft (Series A, $270M).

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses the $100M total raised as a proxy for a $2.0B implied post-money valuation, then applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $3.8B. The peer-median method identifies three same-sector and same/adjacent-stage comparables with a median valuation of $500M and a range spanning $270M to $1.8B.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $425M (low) to $4.4B (high), with a median of $2.1B. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty around stage-appropriate benchmarking in a nascent sector where peer valuations range nearly 7x. Key uncertainties include the relevance of Series C comps to a Series B position, capital efficiency relative to deployed capital, and near-term milestone achievement. The median estimate of $2.1B implies roughly 21x total capital deployed and sits between the peer median and stage-progression methods.
